message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to compile a single crochet round into Crochet IR. Let me analyze the input.

Input round:
- partName: "Assembly"
- rawInstruction: "Sew all parts, nose, eyes and cheeks to finish."
- title: "Sew all parts"

This is an assembly instruction - it's bookkeeping, not stitch-producing. It describes sewing parts together. Let me encode this.

The instruction "Sew all parts, nose, eyes and cheeks to finish." is an assembly instruction. I'll use `semantics = bookkeeping`, `actionTag = assembly`, and put the full instruction text in `instruction`.

There's no explicit stitch count given, so `expectedProducedStitches` = null.

Let me construct the JSON:

```json
{
  "rounds": [
    {
      "title": "Sew all parts",
      "sourceText": "Sew all parts, nose, eyes and cheeks to finish.",
      "expectedProducedStitches": null,
      "body": {
        "statements": [
          {
            "kind": "operation",
            "sourceText": "Sew all parts, nose, eyes and cheeks to finish.",
            "operation": {
              "semantics": "bookkeeping",
              "actionTag": "assembly",
              "stitch": null,
              "count": 1,
              "instruction": "Sew all parts, nose, eyes and cheeks to finish.",
              "target": null,
              "note": null,
              "notePlacement": "all",
              "producedStitches": null
            },
            "repeat": null,
            "conditional": null,
            "note": null
          }
        ],
        "sourceText": null,
        "normalizationNote": null
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

That looks correct. This is a simple bookkeeping operation for assembly.
